meta.title: Nimbus i18n provider
meta.description: The NimbusI18nProvider component provides internationalization (i18n) context for React Aria components in your Nimbus application. It controls how dates, numbers, and other locale-specific content is formatted across all React Aria-based components.

mdx:
## Overview

NimbusI18nProvider is a proxy component that wraps React Aria's I18nProvider,
making it easy to set the locale for all React Aria components like DatePicker,
Calendar, and NumberInput. This ensures consistent formatting across your
application.

### Key features

- **Locale-aware formatting**: Controls date, number, and currency formatting
  based on locale
- **React Aria integration**: Built specifically for React Aria components
- **BCP47 locale codes**: Supports standard language-region codes (e.g.,
  'en-US', 'de-DE')
- **Type-safe**: Full TypeScript support
- **Automatic propagation**: Locale settings cascade to all child React Aria
  components

### Resources

[React Aria I18nProvider Docs](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/I18nProvider.html)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Basic usage

Wrap your application with NimbusI18nProvider to set the locale for React Aria
components:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <NimbusI18nProvider locale="en-US">
    <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
      <Text>This app uses US English formatting</Text>
      <DatePicker label="Select Date" />
    </Stack>
  </NimbusI18nProvider>
);
```

### Different locales

Change the locale prop to switch formatting:

```jsx live
const App = () => {
  const [locale, setLocale] = React.useState("en-US");

  return (
    <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
      <Stack direction="row" gap="200" wrap="wrap">
        <Button onClick={() => setLocale("en-US")} size="sm">
          English (US)
        </Button>
        <Button onClick={() => setLocale("en-GB")} size="sm">
          English (UK)
        </Button>
        <Button onClick={() => setLocale("de-DE")} size="sm">
          Deutsch
        </Button>
        <Button onClick={() => setLocale("es-ES")} size="sm">
          Español
        </Button>
        <Button onClick={() => setLocale("fr-FR")} size="sm">
          Français
        </Button>
        <Button onClick={() => setLocale("ja-JP")} size="sm">
          日本語
        </Button>
      </Stack>

      <NimbusI18nProvider locale={locale}>
        <Stack direction="column" gap="400">
          <Text fontWeight="600">Current Locale: {locale}</Text>
          <DatePicker label="Select Date" />
          <NumberInput
            label="Amount"
            defaultValue={1234.56}
            minValue={0}
            formatOptions={{
              style: "currency",
              currency: locale.startsWith("ja") ? "JPY" : locale.startsWith("en-GB") ? "GBP" : "USD"
            }}
          />
        </Stack>
      </NimbusI18nProvider>
    </Stack>
  );
};
```

### Multiple providers

You can nest NimbusI18nProviders to use different locales in different parts of
your app:

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="column" gap="600">
    <NimbusI18nProvider locale="en-US">
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">US Section</Text>
        <DatePicker label="Date" />
      </Stack>
    </NimbusI18nProvider>

    <NimbusI18nProvider locale="de-DE">
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">German Section</Text>
        <DatePicker label="Datum" />
      </Stack>
    </NimbusI18nProvider>

    <NimbusI18nProvider locale="ja-JP">
      <Stack direction="column" gap="200">
        <Text fontWeight="600">Japanese Section</Text>
        <DatePicker label="日付" />
      </Stack>
    </NimbusI18nProvider>
  </Stack>
);
```

## Overview

NimbusI18nProvider is a proxy component that wraps React Aria's I18nProvider,
making it easy to set the locale for all React Aria components like DatePicker,

### Key features

- **Locale-aware formatting**: Controls date, number, and currency formatting

- **React Aria integration**: Built specifically for React Aria components
- **BCP47 locale codes**: Supports standard language-region codes (e.g.,

- **Type-safe**: Full TypeScript support
- **Automatic propagation**: Locale settings cascade to all child React Aria

### Resources

[React Aria I18nProvider Docs](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/I18nProvider.html)

## Variables

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <NimbusI18nProvider locale="en-US">
    <Text>Content with proper locale formatting</Text>
  </NimbusI18nProvider>
);
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Provide valid locale codes:** Always provide a valid BCP47 language tag
  (e.g., `en-US`, `de-DE`, `fr-FR`) to ensure proper formatting and
  pronunciation.
- **Use region-specific codes:** Use region-specific codes when formatting
  differs (e.g., date order, decimal separators, number formatting).
- **Locale-appropriate formatting:** Dates, numbers, and currency are announced
  in locale-appropriate formats by screen readers.
- **Proper pronunciation:** Screen readers use the specified locale for correct
  pronunciation of content.
- **ARIA labels reflect locale conventions:** Ensures ARIA labels and
  announcements follow locale conventions.
- **Test with screen readers:** Always test with actual screen readers to verify
  proper pronunciation and formatting.

## Resources

- [BCP47 Language Tags](https://www.w3.org/International/articles/language-tags/)
- [WCAG 3.1.2 Language of Parts](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/language-of-parts.html)

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

Use NimbusI18nProvider strategically to support international date and number
formatting.

### Best practices

- **Wrap at root level**: Place NimbusI18nProvider at the root of your
  application to provide i18n context to all React Aria components
- **Use BCP47 codes**: Always use proper BCP47 language tags (e.g., 'en-US',
  'de-DE', 'fr-FR')
- **Regional specificity**: Include region codes when formatting differs by
  region (e.g., 'en-US' vs 'en-GB')
- **Dynamic locale**: Allow users to switch locales at runtime for better user
  experience
- **Consistent currency**: When formatting currency, ensure the currency code
  matches the locale expectations

### When to use

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **International applications**: When your app serves users in multiple
  countries
- **Date formatting**: When you need locale-appropriate date formats (MM/DD/YYYY
  vs DD.MM.YYYY)
- **Number formatting**: When you need locale-appropriate number separators
  (1,234.56 vs 1.234,56)
- **Currency formatting**: When displaying prices in different currencies and
  formats
- **React Aria components**: When using DatePicker, Calendar, NumberInput, or
  other React Aria date/number components

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Single locale apps**: If your app only targets one locale, you may not need
  NimbusI18nProvider
- **Text translation**: This component provides locale context for React Aria
  components. Nimbus components use compiled message dictionaries (see
  [i18n guidelines](../../../../docs/file-type-guidelines/i18n.md) for details)
- **Non-React Aria components**: This only affects React Aria components, not
  general HTML or other component libraries
